mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier discounting and liquidity to equities, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month when policy is easing rather than flat or tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS has fallen over the prior 3 months, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals weakening household income expectations and slower risk appetite, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's hit rate is indistinguishable from random at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST is also rising, the combination indicates broad final-demand strength and a supportive growth backdrop for equities, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS up and HOUST up does not outperform either series alone in forecasting 60-day SPX returns, or if joint-trigger returns are not positive on average.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects improving real production and capacity utilization, which should support cyclical earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO upswings fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the forward-return mean is not above zero in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration combined with a higher policy rate tightens real financial conditions and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-rate triggers do not correspond to negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the joint signal loses significance after controlling for each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS series indicates payroll expansion and stabilizing income, which supports consumer spending and should lift S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive after payroll gains, or if PAYEMS changes have no measurable rank correlation with 5-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in GDPC1 signals slowing aggregate demand and weaker earnings momentum, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter when growth decelerates from the prior trend.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines versus the prior quarterly reading
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than 55%, or if the signal is not stronger than a zero-change benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls while RSAFS also slows, the shared weakness points to fading housing-linked and consumer-demand momentum, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls and RSAFS month-over-month growth is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the combined downside condition fails to yield negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of triggered instances, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.
